summary: First backend test iteration for Homesqre MVP. Built /app/backend/tests/test_homesqre_backend.py with 50 pytest tests covering auth (register/verify/login/me/logout/forgot/reset), catalog seeds (banks/amenities/cities/localities), listings CRUD + view counter, projects CRUD + by-slug, public inquiries with duplicate prevention, interior + loan leads, universal search, content CMS, full admin surface (users/listings/projects/banks/amenities/analytics), favourites, and role-based authorization (agent vs other-agent listing, builder vs other-builder project). All 50 tests pass (100% backend success).

minor.issue: Auth precedence reads cookies BEFORE Authorization Bearer header. A stale cookie in a shared HTTP client silently overrides an explicit Bearer token, leading to surprising auth-as-wrong-user. Recommend: prefer Authorization header when present, fall back to cookie.
No brute-force lockout (playbook requires lockout after 5 failed attempts). No rate limit on /auth/* endpoints.
Response leaks email existence via presence/absence of dev_token key. Acceptable for dev, but gate behind ENV flag for prod.
Universal bypass OTP '000000' accepted regardless of stored OTP. Mock by design, but should be ENV-gated.
Always overwrites is_featured to False when not provided in payload (defaults). Should patch only provided keys to avoid accidental unfeature.
No max upload size enforced. Storage will 500 in test env (Emergent object storage init may fail) — known.

allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False, but app sets httpOnly cookies for auth. Cross-origin cookie auth from production frontend will not work — will need to switch to explicit origins + allow_credentials=True or rely solely on Bearer.

action_items: Flip auth precedence in current_user() to prefer Authorization: Bearer over cookie — current ordering causes wrong-user bugs when a client has stale cookies.
Add brute-force lockout (5 failed attempts) on /api/auth/login per security playbook.
ENV-gate the '000000' OTP bypass and the dev_otp/dev_token fields so they are not exposed in production.
Patch-style update for admin /listings/{id}/status and /projects/{id}/status so is_featured is preserved when omitted.
Add password complexity validation on register & reset.
Plan CORS strategy: switch to explicit allowed origins + allow_credentials=True if cookie auth is intended cross-origin; otherwise document Bearer-only.
Consider splitting server.py (1388 lines) into routers (auth, catalog, listings, projects, inquiries, admin, me) for maintainability.
critical_code_review_comments: current_user() cookie-before-Bearer precedence is a footgun (root cause of half my initial test failures before I introduced a stateless session).
server.py at 1388 lines violates the <700 line file-size guideline; split by domain.
@app.on_event is deprecated in FastAPI ≥0.95 — migrate to lifespan context manager.
Raw user-supplied regex in /listings ?q= and /search ?q= — minor ReDoS risk; escape with re.escape() before $regex.
No pagination on list endpoints (just .limit(N)); will scale poorly.
Mongo _id correctly stripped via projections everywhere I tested — verified no _id in 50 responses.
Slug uniqueness enforced via unique index on listings.slug and projects.slug — good.
Inquiry duplicate prevention via mobile+listing/project within 24h — works (verified 409 on retry).
test_credentials: Used seeded creds from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (admin/agent/builder/customer @homesqre.com). All four seeded accounts login successfully.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data needed. Backend seeds on startup: 4 users, 8 banks, 35 amenities, 1 city (Bangalore), 15 localities, 12 listings, 4 projects — all verified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. For frontend testing: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://homesqre-styled.preview.emergentagent.com. Login flows use cookie OR Bearer. IMPORTANT: The backend's current_user() prefers cookie over Bearer — when writing playwright tests that POST /api/auth/login via fetch with credentials:'include', the httpOnly cookie will set automatically, so subsequent calls work cookie-based. The test file at /app/backend/tests/test_homesqre_backend.py uses a NoCookieSession to force Bearer auth (working around the precedence quirk) — keep this in mind if extending the suite.
